reasoning: Iran war identified as largest oil supply disruption in history (per IEA Bloomberg report). DOW down 600 on oil surge. Energy sector (XLE) is direct beneficiary of high oil prices — oil crossing $90/bbl. Opening XLE May 15 $58 calls (64 DTE, delta 0.55) as swing position. Rationale: (1) Strong macro catalyst with geopolitical staying power — Iran conflicts don't resolve in days; (2) Energy companies profit from high oil prices — XLE constituents see revenue expansion; (3) 64 DTE eliminates near-term theta risk; (4) Tight 1.7% bid/ask spread. Risk: De-escalation/ceasefire news could reverse oil spike overnight. Stop at -15% (~$2.48). Target +25-35% ($3.65-$3.95). Position: 15 contracts at $2.925 mid = $4,387 = 4.3% portfolio exposure.

market_data.oil_context: Iran war largest supply disruption in history per IEA
